H1004.strongs_def: a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)
H1004.kjv_def: court, daughter, door, [phrase] dungeon, family, [phrase] forth of, [idiom] great as would contain, hangings, home(born), (winter) house(-hold), inside(-ward), palace, place, [phrase] prison, [phrase] steward, [phrase] tablet, temple, web, [phrase] within(-out).
H1004.pos: proper patrial adjective, adverb, masculine noun
H1004.usage: household(54x), home(25x), within(22x), temple(11x), prison(16x), place(16x), family(3x), dungeon(2x)

H1005.strongs_def: {a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.)}

H1007.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H205 (אָוֶן); house of vanity;

H1008.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H410 (אֵל); house of God;

H1009.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H695 (אֶרֶב) and H410 (אֵל); house of God's ambush;

H1010.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1168 (בַּעַל) and H4583 (מָעוֹן); house of Baal of (the) habitation of (apparently by transposition); or (shorter) בֵּית מְעוֹןlemma בֵּית מעוֹן missing vowel, corrected to בֵּית מְעוֹן; house of habitation of (Baal);
H1010.kjv_def: Beth-baal-meon. Compare H1186 (בַּעַל מְעוֹן) and H1194 (בְּעֹן).

H1011.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1254 (בָּרָא); house of a creative one;

H1012.derivation: probably from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5679 (עֲבָרָה); house of (the) ford;

H1013.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1447 (גָּדֵר); house of (the) wall;

H1014.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the passive participle of H1576 (גְּמוּל); house of (the) weaned;
H1014.strongs_def: Beth-Gamul, a place East of the Jordan

H1015.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the dual of H1690 (דְּבֵלָה); house of (the) two figcakes;
H1015.strongs_def: Beth-Diblathajim, a place East of the Jordan

H1016.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1712 (דָּגוֹן); house of Dagon;
H1016.strongs_def: Beth-Dagon, the name of two places in Palestine

H1017.derivation: patrial from H1008 (בֵּית־אֵל) with the article interposed;
H1017.strongs_def: a Beth-elite, or inhabitant of Bethel

H1018.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H681 (אֵצֶל) with the article interposed; house of the side;

H1019.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1537 (גִּלְגָּל) with the article interposed; house of Gilgal (or rolling);

H1020.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3451 (יְשִׁימַה) with the article interposed; house of the deserts;
H1020.strongs_def: Beth-ha-Jeshimoth, a town East of the Jordan

H1021.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3754 (כֶּרֶם) with the article interposed; house of the vineyard;

H1022.derivation: patrial from H1035 (בֵּית לֶחֶם) with the article inserted;
H1022.strongs_def: a Beth-lechemite, or native of Bethlechem

H1023.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4801 (מֶרְחָק) with the article interposed; house of the breadth;

H1024.derivation: or (shortened) בֵּית מַרְכָּבוֹת; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H4818 (מֶרְכָּבָה) (with or without the article interposed); place of (the) chariots;
H1024.strongs_def: Beth-ham-Markaboth or Beth-Markaboth, a place in Palestine

H1025.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6010 (עֵמֶק) with the article interposed; house of the valley;

H1026.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6160 (עֲרָבָה) with the article interposed; house of the Desert;

H1027.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7311 (רוּם) with the article interposed; house of the height;
H1027.strongs_def: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan

H1028.strongs_def: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan

H1029.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7848 (שִׁטָּה) with the article interposed; house of the acacia;

H1030.derivation: patrial from H1053 (בֵּית שֶׁמֶשׁ) with the article inserted;
H1030.strongs_def: a Beth-shimshite, or inhabitant of Bethshemesh

H1031.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the same as H2295 (חׇגְלָה); house of a partridge;

H1032.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H2356 (חוֹר); house of hollowness;
H1032.strongs_def: Beth-Choron, the name of two adjoining places in Palestine

H1033.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3733 (כַּר); house of pasture;

H1034.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3833 (לָבִיא); house of lionesses;

H1035.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3899 (לֶחֶם); house of bread;

H1036.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the feminine of H6083 (עָפָר) (with preposition interposed); house to (i.e. of) dust;

H1037.derivation: or בֵּית מִלֹּא; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4407 (מִלּוֹא); house of (the) rampart;
H1037.strongs_def: Beth-Millo, the name of two citadels

H1038.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); house of Maakah;

H1039.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the feminine of H5246 (נָמֵר); house of (the) leopard;
H1039.strongs_def: Beth-Nimrah, a place east of the Jordan

H1040.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5730 (עֵדֶן); house of pleasure;

H1041.strongs_def: house of Azmaveth, a place in Palestine

H1042.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a plural from H6030 (עָנָה); house of replies;

H1043.derivation: an orthographical variation for H1042 (בֵּית עֲנוֹת);

H1044.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative of H6123 (עָקַד); house of (the) binding (for sheep-shearing);

H1045.derivation: ; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת); house of Ashtoreths;
H1045.kjv_def: house of Ashtaroth. Compare H1203 (בְּעֶשְׁתְּרָה), H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת).

H1046.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6412 (פָּלִיט); house of escape;

H1047.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6465 (פְּעוֹר); house of Peor;
H1047.strongs_def: Beth-Peor, a place East of the Jordan

H1048.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative from H6327 (פּוּץ); house of dispersion;

H1049.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6697 (צוּר); house of (the) rock;
H1049.pos: noun, proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H1050.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7339 (רְחֹב); house of (the) street;

H1051.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7497 (רָפָא); house of (the) giant;

H1052.derivation: or בֵּית שָׁן; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7599 (שָׁאַן); house of ease;
H1052.strongs_def: Beth-Shean or Beth-Shan, a place in Palestine

H1053.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8121 (שֶׁמֶשׁ); house of (the) sun;

H1054.derivation: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8598 (תַּפּוּחַ); house of (the) apple;

H1057.strongs_def: the weeping tree (some gum-distilling tree, perhaps the balsam)

H1058.kjv_def: [idiom] at all, bewail, complain, make lamentation, [idiom] more, mourn, [idiom] sore, [idiom] with tears, weep.

H1062.strongs_def: the firstling of man or beast; abstractly primogeniture

H1065.strongs_def: a weeping; by analogy, a dripping

H1066.derivation: plural active participle of H1058 (בָּכָה); (with the article) the weepers;

H1069.derivation: a primitive root; properly, to burst the womb, i.e. (causatively) bear or make early fruit (of woman or tree); also (as denominative from H1061 (בִּכּוּר))
H1069.kjv_def: make firstborn, be firstling, bring forth first child (new fruit).

H1070.derivation: from H1069 (בָּכַר) (in the sense of youth);

H1071.strongs_def: Beker, the name of two Israelites

H1076.strongs_def: a Bakrite (collectively) or descendants of Beker

H1077.strongs_def: properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually (adverb) not at all; also lest
H1077.kjv_def: lest, neither, no, none (that...), not (any), nothing.

H1078.strongs_def: Bel, the Baal of the Babylonians

H1079.strongs_def: properly, anxiety, i.e. (by implication) the heart (as its seat)

H1080.derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה) (but used only in a mental sense);

H1081.derivation: from H1078 (בֵּל) and H113 (אָדוֹן) (contracted); Bel (is his) lord;
H1081.strongs_def: Baladan, the name of a Babylonian prince

H1082.strongs_def: to break off or loose (in a favorable or unfavorable sense), i.e. desist (from grief) or invade (with destruction)

H1083.strongs_def: Bilgah, the name of two Israelites

H1086.strongs_def: to fail; by implication to wear out, decay (causatively, consume, spend)
H1086.kjv_def: consume, enjoy long, become (make, wax) old, spend, waste.

H1089.derivation: a primitive root (rather by transposition for H926 (בָּהַל));

H1090.strongs_def: Bilhah, the name of one of Jacob's concubines; also of a place in Palestine
H1090.pos: proper feminine noun, proper locative noun

H1092.strongs_def: Bilhan, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite

H1093.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה);

H1095.strongs_def: Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel

H1097.strongs_def: properly, failure, i.e. nothing or destruction; usually (with preposition) without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
H1097.kjv_def: corruption, ig(norantly), for lack of, where no...is, so that no, none, not, un(awares), without.
